item.title: Concomitant psoriasis may be associated with a more severe disease course in inflammatory bowel disease: a retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Scientific reports - Psoriasis and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) share genetic and immunological features, but the impact of concomitant psoriasis on the clinical course of IBD is not well defined. We investigated w...

item.title: Anti-inflammatory effects of GLP1-RA drugs.
item.description: The Journal of clinical endocrinology and metabolism - Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1RA) are established treatment options for type 2 diabetes and obesity. This class of medications has redefined care by lowering glucose, reducing bod...

item.title: Chronic Liver Disease, Liver Damage and Liver Failure After Hypoabsorptive Bariatric Surgery: A Dose-Dependent Relationship and Multisystemic Consequences.
item.description: European journal of clinical investigation - Review - Metabolic bariatric surgery remains the most potent weapon we have against severe obesity and its metabolic consequences. Yet, its effects on the liver are far from uniform. Although restrictive an...

item.title: Chronic opioid use and the risk of infections and death in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a nationwide cohort study based on real-world data.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Chronic opioid use (COU) is frequent in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), but data on its association with infections and mortality are scarce. We examined (1) hospital-diagnosed infections, (2) pr...

item.title: Glucocorticoid use and candidemia: results from a nationwide case-control study.
item.description: Clinical microbiology and infection : the official publication of the European Society of Clinical Microbiology and Infectious Diseases - Glucocorticoids are widely used for acute and chronic diseases. Although their association with increased infection risk is well-established, population-level data on glucocorticoid use and candide...

item.title: Epidemiology of diagnosed inflammatory bowel disease in Taiwan population from 2011 to 2021: a population-based study.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) has shown a steadily rising prevalence in Asian populations over recent decades, coinciding with rapid industrialization, Westernized dietary habits, and lifestyle ...

item.title: IBD in transition: a decade of data from the Malaysian Kinta Valley cohort.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is emerging in the Asia-Pacific region including Malaysia. The aim of this study is to compare the current incidence and prevalence of IBD in Malaysia from the prev...

item.title: Mid- to Long-Term Outcomes of the MESOCOLIC Trial Evaluating Extensive Versus Limited Mesenteric Excision in Crohn's Disease.
The Prevalence of Fibromyalgia in the General Population and At-Risk Subpopulations: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Anesthesia and analgesia - Estimates of fibromyalgia prevalence vary widely worldwide due to differences in diagnostic criteria, study methodology, and population characteristics. Accurate estimates are essential to inform p...

item.title: Evaluation of the Anti-Inflammatory Activity of Selected Plant Extracts in an In Vitro Model of Inflammation Using LPS-Stimulated Macrophages.
item.description: Biomedicines - Background: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a group of chronic gastrointestinal (GI) diseases with complex and multifactorial pathop...

item.title: Association between physical activity and inflammatory bowel disease: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: Frontiers in public health - Evidence regarding the association between physical activity (PA) and the long-term incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains limited and inconsistent. This study aimed to evaluate the ...
